content.text: Method 3.1 Integration by Substitution involves three basic steps:
1. Choose a substitution, u(x) that transforms a ‘difficult’ looking integral
to an equivalent but simpler integral
2. Evaluate the resulting expression for the integral in terms of u.
3. Re-substitute for u(x) in terms of x.
This approach is formalised using the chain rule for differentiation; however, in practice we learn what types of substitution are effective by experience.
